description: Model must analyse a failed API call, classify the error, decide whether to retry or abort, specify a backoff strategy if retrying, draft a user-facing status message, and set the appropriate log level. Tests error-recovery reasoning across multiple output fields with enum and numeric constraints.
prompt:
You are an error-recovery controller. A downstream API call has failed. Analyse the failure details below and return a structured recovery plan as a JSON object.

Failure details:
- Endpoint: POST /api/payments/charge
- HTTP status: 429
- Response body: {"error": "rate_limit_exceeded", "retry_after_ms": 2000}
- Attempt number: 2 of 3
- Last attempt timestamp: 2026-04-26T14:33:01Z
- Context: User is waiting for a payment confirmation in a checkout flow

Return a JSON object with: error_class (one of: transient, permanent, rate_limit, auth, unknown), action (one of: retry, abort, fallback, escalate), retry_strategy (object with max_attempts as integer 1–10, initial_delay_ms as non-negative integer, and backoff as one of: linear, exponential, none — include only if action is retry, omit otherwise), user_message (string max 200 chars: what to show the user right now), and log_level (one of: debug, info, warn, error, critical).
